greeting: Morning, Haniel. Wednesday — let's get CT Pros across the finish line.
hype: CT Pros goes live today if you execute clean: company info, fresh QB, catalog scrubbed, then a real deploy. That's a client shipped by end of day. And while DNS propagates, you close the Module pre-mockup gate — settings UI done, branding fixed, audit log wired. Two fronts, one relentless push. Go.
notice: No meetings today — full clear runway.

schedule.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
Re-curate material catalog for a GC
Deploy CT Pros to its own host/domain
Fix branding destructive partial-patch + dirty-gate Save
Wire real audit logging for access + config changes

tasks.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
tasks.notes: config/company.php + .env still hold placeholders inherited from the clone. Set real CT Pros name/address/phone/email BEFORE any client-facing PDF or email goes out.

prep.what: Replace every HighTech placeholder in `config/company.php` and `.env` with CT Pros's real business name, address, phone, and email.
prep.understand: The codebase is a HighTech Tile fork. Every client-facing output — proposal letterheads, estimate PDFs, email footers — pulls company identity from `config/company.php`. Until this is correct, every document a CT Pros client sees carries HighTech branding. Quick task but it gates all client-facing go-live testing.
approach: Open `config/company.php` and replace every field: name, legal_name, address, city, state, zip, phone, email
Check `.env` for MAIL_FROM_NAME, MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS, APP_NAME and update those to CT Pros values
Grep the repo for 'HighTech' in `config/` and `resources/` to catch any stragglers
Hit a `/estimates/{id}/pdf` or `/proposals/{id}/pdf` route to visually confirm CT Pros info appears in the document header
resources: `config/company.php`
`.env` (MAIL_FROM_NAME, MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS, APP_NAME)
CT Pros's real business registration info (have it in front of you before you start)
An estimate or proposal PDF route for the smoke-test

tasks.title: Connect CT Pros's own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
tasks.notes: Inherited tokens were HighTech's and were never carried over. Run a fresh OAuth connect against CT Pros's own QBO company so all syncs hit the right books.

prep.what: Run a fresh QuickBooks OAuth flow authenticated with CT Pros's own QBO credentials — replacing any stale HighTech tokens.
prep.understand: The full QB OAuth infrastructure is built: QuickBooksAuthController, token exchange, encrypted storage, refresh logic — all done. What's missing is running the flow against CT Pros's actual QBO account. Also check `.env`: QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID / SECRET might still be HighTech's Intuit Developer app credentials. Fix that first or the redirect will land on the wrong app.
approach: Check `.env` for QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID / QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET — if HighTech's, go to developer.intuit.com, create the CT Pros Intuit Developer app, copy the new keys, update `.env`
Navigate to Settings → QuickBooks and disconnect any stale existing connection
Hit `/auth/quickbooks/redirect` as super-admin, complete the OAuth dance logged in to the CT Pros QBO account
Verify the `quickbooks_tokens` table has a fresh `access_token` with the CT Pros company ID; run `php artisan quickbooks:sync` and confirm no errors
resources: developer.intuit.com (Intuit Developer Portal — create/find the CT Pros app)
`.env` QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID / QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET
`/auth/quickbooks/redirect` and `/auth/quickbooks/callback` routes
`QuickBooksToken` model and `QuickBooksService` in `app/Services/QuickBooks/`
CT Pros's QBO login credentials (required — have them ready before starting)

tasks.title: Re-curate material catalog for a GC
tasks.notes: The 148-SKU HighTech TILE catalog still seeds — wrong domain. Replace with a general-contractor-appropriate catalog before takeoff pricing or estimates are trusted.

prep.what: Replace the 148-item tile catalog seed with a lean GC-appropriate catalog so estimate line items and Takeoff pricing reflect what CT Pros actually bids.
prep.understand: The catalog drives the estimate line-item picker and the Takeoff → estimate seam. It currently seeds tile SKUs (grout, adhesive, tile patterns) — completely wrong domain. CT Pros is a GC: their catalog needs framing lumber, concrete, drywall, hardware, labor by trade. You don't need real pricing yet (CT Pros fills that in); you need the right categories and unit types. Target 30–50 SKUs.
approach: Find the catalog seeder in `database/seeders/` — open it, read the schema fields (category, name, unit_type, unit_cost, etc.)
Draft GC categories: Concrete & Masonry, Framing Lumber, Sheathing, Drywall & Insulation, Roofing, Rough Electrical, Rough Plumbing, Hardware & Fasteners, Doors & Windows, Labor by trade
Write the replacement seeder with placeholder unit costs; run `php artisan db:seed --class=MaterialCatalogSeeder` on dev DB and confirm it loads cleanly
Open the estimate line-item picker and the Takeoff module → verify new GC items appear, tile categories are gone
resources: Catalog seeder in `database/seeders/` (find by name — MaterialCatalogSeeder or similar)
CT Pros's actual scope of work and typical job types (GC — what trades do they manage?)
Takeoff → estimate seam at `estimates/{estimate}/seed-from-takeoff` — check what catalog fields it expects
Any existing estimate in the app to see the required line-item schema

tasks.title: Deploy CT Pros to its own host/domain
tasks.notes: Deploy infra exists and is proven (deploy/*.sh, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) but is still pointed at the cloned app (sys.hightechtileinc.com / droplet 104.248.228.169). Re-target to a fresh DigitalOcean droplet or Hostinger with a fresh DB and CT Pros's real domain.

prep.what: Provision a new server, point CT Pros's domain to it, and run the proven deploy scripts to stand up a clean CT Pros instance with a fresh database.
prep.understand: The deploy infrastructure is battle-tested from the HighTech deploy: `deploy/*.sh`, `docker-compose.yml`, `docs/deployment.md`. This is a re-targeting job — same scripts, new destination. Critical sequence: provision → DNS → .env → deploy → migrate → smoke. The whole go-live collapses if DNS isn't pointing at the new server before certbot runs for SSL. Give DNS at least 15–30 min to propagate before running certbot.
approach: Provision a fresh Ubuntu 22.04 VPS (DigitalOcean or Hostinger) — note the IP; SSH in; run the server bootstrap portion of `deploy/setup.sh`
Update CT Pros's domain DNS A record to point at the new IP — do this early, DNS takes time
Clone the ct-pros repo to `/opt/ct-pros`; copy `.env` from the HighTech template and change EVERY HighTech-specific value: APP_URL, APP_KEY, DB credentials, MAIL config, QB keys, company info
Once DNS resolves, run `deploy/deploy.sh` — confirm it completes cleanly; then `php artisan migrate --seed` for a fresh DB
Smoke test: open the domain, log in as super-admin, create a test estimate, verify PDF generation and email delivery work
resources: `deploy/*.sh` and `docker-compose.yml` (proven scripts — read before running)
`docs/deployment.md` (step-by-step guide written for this exact flow)
CT Pros's domain registrar credentials (to update the DNS A record)
New server SSH access
HighTech `.env` as the template — with a checklist of every value that must change for CT Pros

tasks.title: Per-module settings UI (labels, statuses, options)
tasks.notes: Config form per module reading the settings layer — rename labels, define statuses/pipeline stages, edit seedable option lists. Zero-hardcode in practice.

prep.what: Build a per-module config form in the Settings panel — the screen where a super-admin renames labels, defines pipeline statuses, and edits option lists for any module without touching code.
prep.understand: The `SettingsManager` (t2) and the Module Manager grid (t8) are both done. The missing piece is the per-module config form — the screen you get when you click into a module's settings and change its labels. This is the zero-hardcode promise made real: a new client rebrands from the UI, not a code fork. It also unblocks t11 (the UX-max pass on the module manager). The UX spec is in `.claude/docs/ux-kb/04-settings.md`.
approach: Read `.claude/docs/ux-kb/04-settings.md` (Labels & Statuses section) — that's your design reference for the form layout
Create `ModuleSettingsController` with `show()` reading `SettingsManager::getModule($key)` and `update()` calling `setMany()`
Build `Pages/Settings/ModuleSettings.vue` — Inertia page rendering each setting as the right input type (text input, status-list builder, option picker)
Add a 'Configure' gear-icon link on each module card in the Module Manager grid routing to `ModuleSettingsController@show` for that module key
resources: `app/Settings/SettingsManager.php` — the read/write API to call
`.claude/docs/ux-kb/04-settings.md` — Settings UX spec (Labels & Statuses section)
`resources/js/Pages/Settings/` — existing pages to match the layout pattern
Module Manager page (t8 source) — the navigation entry point to link from
`ModuleRegistry` — for the full list of module keys to route against

tasks.title: Fix branding destructive partial-patch + dirty-gate Save
tasks.notes: branding.update is a full-replace masquerading as PATCH: a name-only save silently NULLs tagline/trust/remember_default. Use 'sometimes' update semantics so omitted fields are untouched, and gate Save on dirty state.

prep.what: Make the branding PATCH non-destructive (omitting a field leaves it unchanged in the DB) and disable Save until something actually changes.
prep.understand: The bug: `branding.update` uses `?? '' / ?? false` null-coalescing on all fields. A save with only the name field filled silently NULLs tagline, trust-line, and remember_default in the database. Silent data-loss. The second issue: the Save button fires immediately with no changes — wasteful and confusing. Both are quick fixes but both are real bugs affecting every clone using the branding feature.
approach: Open `BrandingController::update()` — switch optional field validation to `'sometimes'` rules so Laravel only validates and updates fields present in the request
Or explicitly call `$settings->set($key, $value)` only for keys where `$request->has($key)` is true
In `Branding.vue`, capture the initial loaded values on mount; compute `isDirty` via a deep-compare against current form state; bind the Save button's `disabled` to `!isDirty`
Test: load the form, change only the name, save → verify tagline and trust-line retain their previous values in the database
resources: `app/Http/Controllers/Settings/BrandingController.php`
`resources/js/Pages/Settings/Branding.vue`
Laravel `'sometimes'` validation rule (validate/update only if field is present in request)
Inertia `useForm()` — check if it has a built-in `isDirty` before writing your own

tasks.title: Wire real audit logging for access + config changes
tasks.notes: BLOCKER: syncRoles/syncPermissions write the pivot directly and fire NO model events. is_active toggles and Setting/ModuleState writes also emit zero audit data. Needs explicit activity() logging. The spec mandates a 'who changed which permission / who deactivated whom' trail — mockups assume that data exists.

prep.what: Add explicit Spatie ActivityLog entries for role/permission sync writes, module enable/disable toggles, and Settings changes — so the system has a real who-changed-what audit trail.
prep.understand: `syncRoles()`/`syncPermissions()` write directly to Spatie's pivot table with no Eloquent model events. `LogsActivity` on the Role model never fires for these. Same for module `is_active` toggles (direct `ModuleState::update`) and `SettingsManager::set()`. The UX spec and Phase 9 audit requirements assume this timeline data exists — currently it produces nothing. Mockups cannot be built on top of a feature that leaves no trail.
approach: In `RoleController` (the permission-sync route): snapshot `$role->permissions->pluck('name')` before the sync, call `syncPermissions()`, then log: `activity()->performedOn($role)->causedBy(auth()->user())->withProperties(['before' => $before, 'after' => $role->fresh()->permissions->pluck('name')])->log('permissions.synced')`

In `ModuleState` enable/disable (wherever `is_active` is toggled): `activity()->performedOn($moduleState)->causedBy(auth()->user())->withProperties(['module' => $moduleState->module_key])->log($enabled ? 'module.enabled' : 'module.disabled')`
In `SettingsManager::set()` and `setMany()`: log the key(s), old value(s), new value(s), and `auth()->user()` as causer
Write one Pest test per site asserting an `Activity` row exists with the correct subject, causer, and log name after the action fires
resources: Spatie ActivityLog manual logging docs — `activity()->on($model)->causedBy($user)->withProperties([])->log('event')`
`app/Http/Controllers/RoleController.php` — find the permission-sync route
`app/Models/ModuleState.php` — find the enable/disable toggle method
`app/Settings/SettingsManager.php` — `set()` and `setMany()` methods
Existing `LogsActivity` usage in the codebase for reference patterns
